title: Cay Qel-Droma
content:

{{Quote|By the ghost of Freedon Nadd! He's just one man! Can't you defeat one man?|[[Novar (Human)|Novar]], to the troops of Iziz, concerning Cay Qel-Droma|audio=CayQD1-TOTJ.flac|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi audio">[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
'''Cay Qel-Droma''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] during the events of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Born on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] to a [[Qel-Droma family|family with Jedi heritage]], Qel-Droma and his older brother, fellow [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], were instructed by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] at [[Arca Jeth's praxeum|his training compound]] on [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]]. Qel-Droma's first mission came in [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], when, along with his brother and fellow trainee [[Tott Doneeta]], he was sent to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] to put down an [[Battle of Iziz|uprising]] of the [[Beast Riders]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] wilderness against the walled [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]]. The Jedi succeeded, but Qel-Droma's career on the fields of battle had just begun.

He helped quell the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising|Naddist Uprising]] started by a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Sith cult/Legends|cult]] on Onderon, and later fought in the Great Sith War, a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy-spanning]] conflict that started when the dark side organization known as the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] took control of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]]. During the war, Ulic turned to the dark side, becoming a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], and second-in-command under [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]. Though Kun attempted to lure Qel-Droma to the dark side as well, he could not be tempted, and remained a Jedi, a proud defender of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Qel-Droma fought opposite his brother in battles such as the [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|Battle of Coruscant]]. In [[3996 BBY]] Qel-Droma finally came face-to-face with Ulic, [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|dueling]] him on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. Ulic killed his brother, though he regretted the act enough to abandon the dark side. Filled with guilt, Ulic lived the rest of his life a broken man.

==Biography==
===Early life and training===
Cay Qel-Droma was born on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] to the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Lien-Tsai Qel-Droma]]. He was very skilled in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], but his mother did not train him, believing that she would be unable to maintain the emotional separation required to train Cay, and his older brother, [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]], as [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Together with Ulic, Cay Qel-Droma was sent to [[Arca Jeth]]'s [[Arca Jeth's praxeum|Jedi training center]] on [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]] for further instruction. Even at a young age, the two boys were the best of friends.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" /> Alongside the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Tott Doneeta]], the two learned the ways of the Force from the venerated Jedi Master. The three young Jedi learned much from the respected Master, such as [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] and different Force skills. Cay Qel-Droma also advanced his love for machines by frequently repairing any droids or other machines that had bugs.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" /> During their training, Cay began to grow concerned for Ulic as his older brother grew frustrated when he failed at something. He attempted to voice his concerns through jokes, although Ulic remained very intense about training.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" />

===First mission===
{{Quote|Nice piece of work, Ulic! Between us and the palace guard, we got 'em licked!|Cay Qel-Droma|audio=WeGotEmLickedfull-TOTJ.flac|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi audio" />}}
In [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], Jeth was chosen as the [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi Watchman]] of the [[Japrael system/Legends|Onderon system]]. He hoped to resolve the centuries-long [[Beast Wars]] that had plagued [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. However, he was not to go himself. Instead, he sent his three apprentices on their first real test—a mission with lives hanging in the balance. Onderon was home to many savage beasts, some of which had been tamed by outlaws known as [[Beast Riders]]. These beasts had forced the other citizens of Onderon into one giant walled [[City/Legends|city]], [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]], which was constantly under attack from the Beast Riders, who had been at war with the monarchy of the city for centuries.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

[[File:CayOnArkania.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Cay Qel-Droma tinkering with a droid, an activity he loved.]]
When the three Jedi arrived in the Qel-Dromas' [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Nebulon Ranger]]'', they were attacked by a group of Beast Riders on their [[drexl]] mounts. However, due to the expert [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] skills of Doneeta and the Qel-Droma brothers, the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was able to land in Iziz intact. Once in the city, they introduced themselves to a group of security officers sent to welcome them, only to see Tott Doneeta nearly arrested, as the city had a policy against any [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Furious, the brothers drew their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], and the guards reluctantly released Doneeta. The three were then led to the throne room of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Amanoa]].<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

The Jedi were introduced to the queen and her daughter, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Galia]]. Seconds later, a group of Beast Riders swept into the palace, breaking the [[Glass/Legends|glass]] [[Window/Legends|windows]] and landing in the throne room. The trio fought the Riders alongside the guards, but they were unable to stop them from kidnapping Galia. After they had taken her, the Riders retreated. Amanoa was furious at the Jedi, claiming that they had aided the Beast Riders. Ulic promised that the three of them would rescue her daughter, insisting that they would not return until they had freed her.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

Flying above the forests, the ''Nebulon Ranger'' was fired on by Beast Riders, and crashed. Though the three Jedi survived, they would have to make it to the Beast Riders' fortress on foot. On their way to the Riders' citadel, they were attacked by [[Boma]] beasts. However, with Doneeta's ability to [[Beast Language|speak]] beast languages, he was able to [[Animal bond|make]] the creatures halt, giving he and his companions a ride to the fortress. Once there, they crashed in, interrupting the [[Binding Ceremony of Oron Kira and Galia|wedding]] between Galia and the Beast Riders' second-in-command, [[Oron Kira]], son of [[Chief/Legends|chief]] [[Modon Kira]]. Though Ulic attempted to save Galia, he was stunned when she told him that she did not want to be rescued. Hoping to make peace with the Jedi, Kira invited them to put down their weapons and join the wedding banquet.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

During the banquet, Kira explained that he had a secret route into Iziz, and on one of his undercover trips he had encountered Galia. The two had fallen in love, but since the queen would never permit her daughter to marry a Beast Rider, Galia had arranged to be "kidnapped." She told the Jedi that she wished to escape her parents, who were practitioners of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Kira explained that four hundred years before, a rogue Jedi named [[Freedon Nadd]] had brought knowledge of the dark side to Onderon. A [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]], Nadd had ruled the planet for years before his [[Death/Legends|death]]. The rulers after him learned his teachings, and [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] were passed down from generation to generation between the [[King/Legends|kings]] and queens, who continued Nadd's legacy.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

===Encounter with the dark side===
{{Quote|I call upon the spirit of Freedon Nadd, a Jedi far greater than these impotent youths. May the dark power that he taught me and my husband and all our ancestors fill me now!|Queen Amanoa|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />}}
[[File:CayLosesHisArm.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Cay Qel-Droma, after losing his arm to a palace guard]]
Kira pleaded that the Jedi help them defeat Amanoa and her husband, King [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]], a fellow [[darksider]]. The Jedi, with Ulic as their spokesman, agreed. As Kira gathered all the other Beast Lords and their Riders, the ''Nebulon Ranger'' headed back to Iziz, escorted by drexls. The ship landed in the city, and the Jedi headed to the palace with Galia. However, Amanoa did not give them a chance to talk, cursing her daughter for her betrayal. Then, claiming the darkness inside her would destroy the Jedi, she called for Freedon Nadd, unleashing her rage and creating a blanket of darkness in the palace. Realizing his allies had failed in their attempt at a peaceful solution, Kira called for the Riders to attack, soon swarming the city.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

In the palace, the Jedi were fighting a group of security guards. Unable to see due to the darkness, Cay Qel-Droma was shot in the hand by a guard. Unable to defend himself, he could not prevent a guard from cutting off his left [[Limb/Legends|arm]]. Grabbing his brother, Ulic fled from the throne room, away from the skirmish. Cay Qel-Droma survived thanks to a [[medkit]], and he bonded an [[XT-6 droid|XT-6]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] arm to his body to serve as a [[Cybernetics/Legends|replacement limb]].<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

Though they fought hard, the Beast Riders were unable to best the soldiers due to Amanoa's use of the dark side—using a form of [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], she bolstered the morale of her forces while weakening that of the Riders. However, the tide turned upon the arrival of a strange starship carrying Jedi Master Arca Jeth, who had arrived to aid his apprentices. With his own battle meditation he was able to negate Amanoa's abilities and encourage the Riders to fight on. Though Jeth was at first upset with the three Jedi, he admitted that perhaps this had been too great a test for them.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

Jeth led the Jedi to the [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd]], where Amanoa hid. Upon exposure to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]], she was weakened, and died. Jeth showed the group Nadd's remains, and explained that the dark side's hold over Onderon was broken. In the following days, Galia and Kira were made rulers of Onderon. However, the Jedi could still feel the darkness, and Jeth believed that some supporters of Nadd still lived.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" />

===Naddist Uprising===
[[File:NullvsJedi.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Jedi dueling Warb Null]]
{{Quote|Something terrible is at work!|Arca Jeth|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising]]''</ref>}}
During the funeral procession of Queen Amanoa and Freedon Nadd, in which the remains of the beings would be transferred to a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of Onderon, the darksiders struck. Using a [[groundborer]], a group of [[Naddist]]s drilled their way to the surface, interrupting the funeral. A mysterious [[Armor/Legends|armor]]-clad [[darksider]], [[Warb Null]], attacked Qel-Droma and the Jedi [[Oss Wilum]], while more Naddists grabbed the sarcophagi of Amanoa and Nadd. Struck by a [[Sith sorcery/Legends#Force Blast|blast]] of dark side energy, Jeth was unable to help. Once they had seized the sarcophagi, the Naddists fled as soon as they had appeared.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising" />

Realizing the situation was desperate, Galia suggested that they see her father, King Ommin, who resided in a secret underground facility. Ulic and Jeth decided to go with Galia, while Qel-Droma, Doneeta, and Willum stayed above to help secure the palace. However, things took a disastrous turn for the Jedi. Ommin was empowered by Freedon Nadd, whose spirit had survived for centuries, spreading the dark side on Onderon. The old man unleashed a wave of dark side energy, paralyzing Jeth. Ulic and Galia were forced to flee, while Jeth was taken [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]] by Ommin and Nadd's spirit. Above, the three Jedi had lost the palace, and, along with Ulic, Galia, and Kira, had to abscond. They decided to head to Oron Kira's fortress, making it a base of operations.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising" />

The Jedi were pinned in the fortress by the Naddists when, suddenly, fortunes changed. A group of Jedi, sent along with the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Fleet]] from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], had arrived to aid those trapped on Onderon. Led by [[Nomi Sunrider]], the Jedi and the Beast Riders [[Second Battle of Onderon|forced]] the Naddists back. Ulic and his companions made their way to Iziz, where they confronted Ommin in his underground lair. Ommin had hung Jeth on the wall as a trophy, and though he still lived, he suffered greatly. During the skirmish, Qel-Droma lost his droid arm to a [[Sith Elite Warbot|Sith War Droid]], though he kept fighting, and would later build another replacement limb for himself. Ulic, determined to rescue his Master from the clutches of Freedon Nadd and his puppet Ommin, fought through the droid ranks before confronting and slicing through the King's body. In his dying moments, Ommin called for Nadd, but the spirit announced that he was abandoning Ommin. Nadd told the Jedi that one day, one of them would belong to the dark side, and they would realize the truth—they had lost. With that bold proclamation, Nadd's spirit departed.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising" />

===Conflict in the Empress Teta system===
{{Quote|I thought the Sith teachings that were active here had been contained. Apparently, I was wrong.|Arca Jeth|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith]]''</ref>}}
[[File:CayUnleashed.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Cay Qel-Droma, discussing plans for opposing the Krath with his fellow Jedi.]]
However, the Jedi's job was not done. Shortly after cleansing Onderon of the dark side, Jeth learned that the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] now faced conflict. A [[Political uprising of the Empress Teta system|political coup]] was apparently related to Freedon Nadd, and so Jeth decided that the Jedi would solve this problem. He informed the Jedi that two youths, [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]] had taken control of the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. More to the point, they had somehow learned the ways of the dark side, forming a secret dark side society known as the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]]. [[Dace Diath]], Oss Wilum, [[Qrrrl Toq]], and [[Shoaneb Culu]] would travel to [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] and attempt to recruit more Jedi, while the others would stay on Onderon. Jeth planned to send Sunrider and Ulic to the system soon after teaching Sunrider more about battle meditation. During their time together, Ulic and Sunrider fell in love.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

Several days later, a [[Jedi archaeologist]] named [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] landed on Onderon, seeking to learn more about the Sith from the artifacts Ommin had owned. Qel-Droma and Tott Doneeta greeted him, although, on Jeth's orders, Qel-Droma told Kun very little. Angered, Kun insisted he be allowed to see the relics, but he was confronted by Jeth, who told Kun he knew he was no archaeologist, and ordered he leave. After Kun departed, Jeth revealed to Qel-Droma and Doneeta that he had sensed Kun's malicious intent, and that nothing good would come of letting him view the Sith artifacts.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

Meanwhile, in the Empress Teta system, a [[Battle of Koros Major|battle]] was raging for [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]], the last Tetan world still opposing the Ketos' coup. Though a joint Jedi-[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] fleet led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Orley Vanicus]], Sunrider, and Ulic arrived, the defenders were still unable to defeat the military forces of the Ketos. Unable to gain an edge, the Republic force retreated. During the battle, Ulic was wounded by a piece of slag from a [[CX-133 Chaos fighter|Chaos fighter]] covered in [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]].<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

Forced to flee the battle, the Jedi now had another destination—[[Deneba/Legends|Deneba]], host to a [[Conclave at Deneba|great meeting]] between Jedi from all across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. On Deneba, Qel-Droma saw his brother again, and was surprised to see him wounded. Ulic explained what had happened to him as the meeting got underway. Jedi Master [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] presided over the meeting, explaining the conclave's purpose—to discuss the recent failure of the task force in the Empress Teta system and what the Jedi could do to defeat the Krath. Odan-Urr then welcomed Ulic, newly appointed Watchman of the Empress Teta system, to the stand. Ulic informed the audience of his plan—to destroy the Krath from within. As he went into detail about his scheme, he was met with resistance from the crowd. Many Jedi, most notably Jeth, opposed Ulic joining the Krath. At this point, Cay Qel-Droma, who also disapproved of Ulic's plan, noticed that the servant droids present at the meeting to aid the Jedi were acting strange, but he dismissed this feeling.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

During Ulic's speech, the Jedi suddenly felt a great [[Disturbance in the Force/Legends|disturbance in the Force]] as a strong pain resonated through all the Jedi. Unknown to anyone on the planet was that the disturbance emanated from a [[Korriban/Legends|distant planet]] by Exar Kun, who had just renounced the light side, joining the Sith. At that moment, a group of ships jumped out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] above Deneba. A flurry of [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] rained down on the planet. They opened to reveal [[Krath war droid/Legends|war droids]], created by the Krath, who had declared war on the Jedi. The servant droids at the meeting suddenly turned on their masters, revealing themselves to have been modified by the Krath to make them Jedi-killers.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

The Jedi engaged the deadly droids in combat, but found them to be nearly their match. Using methods learned during the [[Great Droid Revolution]] [[4015 BBY|eighteen years earlier]], Jeth short-circuited the droids' systems, a skill he planned to teach his apprentices, but he would never get the chance. Attacked from behind by a droid, Arca Jeth, the beloved master of Cay and Ulic Qel-Droma, was killed. Though the Jedi eventually won the day, it was not a complete victory. Ulic was devastated by Jeth's death, blaming himself for not sensing the droid. Distraught, he decided to put his plan in motion, against the advice of many Jedi, including his love, Sunrider, and his brother.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

===A brother's betrayal===
[[File:HuggingBrothers.jpg|right|thumb|The Qel-Droma brothers, prior to Ulic's departure for the Empress Teta system.]]
{{Quote|Cay…Ulic is lost to us.|Nomi Sunrider|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />}}
Promising everything would work out, Ulic left for the Empress Teta system, hoping to convince the Ketos he was a fallen Jedi intending to learn from the two darksiders. The Ketos discovered and [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] him. He was slowly seduced to the dark side, however, eventually losing sight of his original goal. Concerned with Ulic, who they could no longer sense in the Force, Qel-Droma, Sunrider, and Doneeta decided to head to [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]], hoping to find him.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

In keeping with his now all-too-real charade, Ulic imprisoned Sunrider, ordering her to be executed. She was able to escape, sending her coordinates to Qel-Droma and Doneeta to pick her up in the ''Nebulon Ranger''. However, the Jedi were not yet ready to abandon Ulic. Using a secret passage discovered by Sunrider, they made their way into the royal quarters. There, they confronted Satal. However, before he could attack them, Ulic appeared, and lunged for Satal, engaging him in combat. The two dueled, with Ulic, empowered by rage, emerging victorious. Qel-Droma encouraged Ulic to flee with them, but he refused. When Qel-Droma persisted, Ulic flattened his friends with a blast of dark side energy. Despite Sunrider's misgivings, the Jedi retreated. Ulic had changed. He was no longer the brother Qel-Droma had known and loved.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

Back on Ossus, the Jedi planned for their next move against the Krath, still hoping to rescue Ulic. Cay Qel-Droma was involved in testing the new [[Star Saber XC-01 starfighter|Star Saber XC-01]] [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] along with Dace Diath. Following a test flight that seemingly killed Diath, Oron Kira arrived with his Beast Riders. Diath had not died but instead ejected at the last moment and used Force Levitation to save himself after the destruction of his fighter. The Jedi were planning another rescue mission, this time with the help of the Riders and their drexl mounts. However, before they could leave, the Masters wished to speak with them. [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] and [[Thon]] informed the young Jedi that Ulic had to be the one to see the error of his ways, and turn from the dark side under his own volition. If he did not, he would destroy himself, as was the way of the Force. And so, with that fateful message, the Jedi prepared for departure. Flying Star Sabers and [[S-100 Stinger-class starfighter|Stingers]], they headed for Cinnagar, hoping to succeed where Cay Qel-Droma's crew had failed earlier. Once again, Sunrider and Qel-Droma would be the ones who would have the responsibility of speaking to Ulic while the other Jedi and the Beast Riders held the enemy at bay.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

Caught by surprise, the Jedi were able to breach the defenses and fly into the inner city. Disembarking, Qel-Droma, Sunrider and Qrrrl Toq leapt into the [[Iron Citadel/Legends|Iron Citadel]], the capitol building. Though Aleema Keto attempted to use her [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith sorcery]] to force the Jedi to retreat, Sunrider negated her powers with her newly-improved battle meditation. Sunrider then tried to convince Ulic to leave with her, calling on the love the two felt for each other. However, Ulic refused. When Cay Qel-Droma and Sunrider persisted, Ulic attacked them with dark side energy. Toq and Qel-Droma grabbed Ulic, planning to haul him to their ship and force him to come to Ossus with them. They were stopped, however, by Sunrider, who reminded the Jedi what the Masters said—Ulic must renounce the dark side under his own power. Ultimately, Qel-Droma relented and the Jedi left his brother behind. Ulic had changed, as Qel-Droma now realized. He had fallen to the darkness, although Qel-Droma refused to accept the notion that his brother was beyond saving.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

===Great Sith War===
{{Quote|Who else will resist my order?''"<br />"''I will, Ulic.|Ulic and Cay Qel-Droma|<ref name="Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi—The Sith War">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Cayqel-dromaHS.jpg|thumb|left|145px|Cay Qel-Droma, confronting his brother on Coruscant]]
Not long after this, Exar Kun met with several [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]], including Cay Qel-Droma, on Ossus. He attempted to convince them to join him, claiming he had found new methods feared by the Jedi Masters. He had been given great power, he said, and he could teach it to the young Jedi. Cay, however, was skeptical, and questioned Kun. Ultimately, Kun left with several Jedi aboard his starship, although Cay was not among them. Unknown to all of them was that Exar Kun had fallen to the dark side of the Force, and was attempting to subvert them. Kun had also taken Ulic Qel-Droma as his apprentice, and the two were plotting to [[Great Sith War/Legends|conquer]] the galaxy.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

Cay and Ulic were to meet again. While Kun trained his new students on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], the Krath forces, led by Ulic and aided by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|attacked]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], capital of the Galactic Republic. Caught by surprise, the planet was nearly defenseless at first, but the Jedi fought to repel the invaders. As the Republic Fleet arrived, the battle began to turn back to the Jedi's favor. Suddenly, the Krath forces retreated. Aleema Keto betrayed Ulic, her lover, hoping to gain more power for herself. Left alone, Ulic was confronted by the Jedi, including his brother. They arrested Ulic, using a [[Wall of light]] to prevent him from utilizing his dark powers.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

Ulic was [[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma|tried]] before the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] to answer for his crimes, and Cay Qel-Droma was one of the few who spoke in his brother's defense, despite being repeatedly rejected by him. Unfortunately for Qel-Droma, the Senate would not listen to him, and it seemed inevitable that Ulic would be sentenced to death. Ulic also did not want his brother's help, as he cursed the Republic and boasted that soon the Sith would conquer the galaxy. However, the proceedings were interrupted by Exar Kun, who had come to save his apprentice. Cay attempted to stop Ulic, but when left with no choice but to kill his brother, he found himself unable to do it. Frozen by Kun's Sith magic, the Senate's guards were powerless. Cursing the Republic, Kun and Ulic headed for the exit, although Kun was [[Duel in the Senate|confronted]] by Vodo-Siosk Baas, his old master. Kun cut him down without remorse, and the two Sith left the Senate behind. Coruscant remained in the hands of the Republic, but Ulic had escaped, and the Sith remained strong.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

Shortly after this, a Jedi task force encountered a Sith battle group in the [[Cron system]]. However, this was a trap, as the Sith [[Battleship/Legends|battleship]] [[Corsair (Derriphan-class)|''Corsair'']], [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the Sith task force, was ready to destroy the Jedi—and the [[Star/Legends|star]] system. Using ancient magic developed by the Sith Lord [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], the ''Corsair'' tore one of the stars apart in a massive [[Cron Supernova/Legends|supernova]], creating a chain reaction that swallowed the system, killing all in it. The massive shock wave moved on to Ossus, where the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]] was located. However, this had all been planned by Kun. When the Jedi were evacuating, he would sweep in and steal as many artifacts as he could before the firestorm hit.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

As the Jedi began to gather up what they could in the short time they had, Kun and Ulic [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|arrived]]. After raiding the storehouses, the two Sith boarded their ships and took off. However, Ulic was unable to make a clean getaway, as he was attacked by a ship in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] and forced to go evasive. This ship was in fact the ''Nebulon Ranger'', piloted by Cay Qel-Droma, who was still unable to kill his brother and so attempted to disable his ship. With the aid of a Mandalorian on a [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droid]], Ulic gained the upper hand, and in turn attacked his brother. Cay Qel-Droma's ship was damaged and he crashed on Ossus, with Ulic right behind him, ready to fight.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

[[File:Ulicqeldromaversuscay.jpg|thumb|right|210px|The brothers duel]]
Unlike Cay Qel-Droma, Ulic was prepared to kill his brother. Drawing their lightsabers, the two [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|dueled]], with Qel-Droma urging Ulic to rejoin the light. Ulic refused, boasting of his new-found power. Finally, in a fit of rage, he sliced through Qel-Droma's droid arm and his chest, mortally wounding his brother. However, this act shocked Ulic. Horrified at what he had done, Ulic abandoned the dark side, crying for his brother, who died in Ulic's arms. If unsuccessful at redeeming his brother while living, Cay Qel-Droma had saved his brother in death.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Cay…Cay, forgive me!|Ulic Qel-Droma|<ref name="Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi—The Sith War" />}}
Stunned at Ulic's slaying of his brother, Nomi Sunrider used the rarely seen [[Force power/Legends|technique]] Sever Force to permanently blind him from the Force, robbing him of what made him a Jedi. Distraught at the death of his beloved brother and now his inability to feel the Force, Ulic surrendered to the Jedi, a Sith no longer. He led the Jedi to Yavin 4, where Exar Kun was. In the rush to evacuate Ossus, Ulic was careful to take Cay's body with him as he showed the Jedi the way to Yavin 4. There, they were able to defeat Kun, ending the Great Sith War. However, Ulic had been damaged beyond repair. Unable to forgive himself for his misdeeds, he spent the next several years of his life wandering the galaxy in his ship ''[[Cay's Dream]]''.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

Ulic would later encounter visions of Cay Qel-Droma on both Yavin 4 and [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]], echoes of the past caused by his guilt. However, in [[3986 BBY]], he was finally able to pardon himself, thanks to Sunrider's daughter [[Vima Sunrider|Vima]], who became his apprentice of her own volition, seeking to learn about the Force. Shortly after coming to terms with himself, Ulic was shot in the back and mortally wounded by the [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] [[Hoggon]]. However, in Nomi's arms and with Vima at his side, he died in peace and passed into the Force.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Redemption">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Redemption]]''</ref> In the years after Cay's death and the destruction of Ossus at the hands of Exar Kun, the [[Qel-Droma's Grave|spot]] upon which he was slain was marked off by the local [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]] tribesmen with a 30-meter perimeter of large black stones. The spot was also the site of a powerful dark side Force [[Vergence/Legends|vergence]], which was believed to be created by the sundering of Ulic's connection to the Force.<ref name="nexus">''[[Nexus of Power]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Hold to the light, Oss! It's all we've got!|Cay Qel-Droma|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising" />}}
[[File:Cay_qel_droma.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Cay Qel-Droma, with his cybernetic arm]]
Cay Qel-Droma was a kind person. He loved his brother, and was broken up when Ulic turned to the dark side. Growing up, the two were inseparable, and when their mother told Cay that she worried they would develop a sibling rivalry, Cay simply laughed, as the two got along so well. Years later, Cay Qel-Droma's death broke the dark side's hold on Ulic. Cay was also a more positive person than his brother, and did not dwell on things as much, such as when he was unable to complete a task. Unlike Ulic, Cay did not feel a tremendous need to be successful at everything, to avoid failure. He did not worry about living up to his name like his brother did. In fact, this ambition of Ulic's ultimately led to his downfall.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" />

Qel-Droma enjoyed the life of a Jedi, following the path with great pleasure and determination. He was also very fond of his master, Arca Jeth. When Jeth died, Qel-Droma was stunned and greatly hurt, although he was not affected as badly as his brother. He also cared for his fellow Jedi, and was pained whenever one of his friends died. Qel-Droma was especially close with Nomi Sunrider and Tott Doneeta, whom he trained with under Jeth.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" /><ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

He also loved working with machines. He could be found tinkering with a droid frequently, and was reprimanded by Jeth at least once for working on a droid, rather than listening to him speak. Cay's love of machines was epitomized by the fact that when he lost his left arm, he replaced it with a prosthetic droid arm, "welding" it to his body. Even as a boy, he had a tremendous interest in machines, and could be found simply watching speeders dock at the port on Alderaan for hours. He was more mechanically minded than his brother, who focused more on the Force and becoming a stronger Jedi, although Qel-Droma certainly was dedicated to the Jedi. Like Ulic, Cay used a green lightsaber.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" /><ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" /><ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" />

==Powers and abilities==
[[File:CayQD_saber.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Cay Qel-Droma's lightsaber]]
Cay Qel-Droma was strong in the light side of the Force. He was also a talented duelist, nearly the equal of his brother, who was regarded as one of the most powerful Jedi of his time.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" /> He was a master of [[Form IV/Legends|Form IV: Ataru]].<ref name="JATM">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref> He was also perceptive, as evidenced by the fact that he was the only one to sense the servant droids' true intent on Deneba. He was talented at fixing machines. The Jedi was a talented pilot as well, and was selected to test fly the Star Saber starfighter due to his piloting skills.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" /><ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon" /><ref name="Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith" />

==Behind the scenes==
Cay Qel-Droma was created for the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series by [[Tom Veitch]]. Unlike Ulic, Cay Qel-Droma did not originate in the [[Dark Empire endnotes|''Dark Empire'' endnotes]], and was created when ''Tales of the Jedi'' began. He first appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon|''Ulic Qel-Droma and the Beast Wars of Onderon'']] as one of the main protagonists, and later reappeared in three more story arcs as one of the major heroes. Qel-Droma's role is that of a hero; he supports the Jedi throughout the series and combats the darksiders. His most important act comes in redeeming Ulic from the dark side of the Force.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War" /> The character was voiced by [[Skip Lackey]] in the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]],<ref name="Tales of the Jedi audio" /> and by [[Jack Noseworthy]] in the [[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Dark Lords of the Sith'' arc audio drama]].<ref name="Dark Lords of the Sith audio drama">[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Dark Lords of the Sith'' arc audio drama]]</ref> He was later given a [[Star Wars Miniatures|''Miniatures'']] figure in the [[Masters of the Force (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Masters of the Force'']] set, released in [[April]] [[2010]].<ref name="Miniatures">{{SWMiniCite|set=Masters of the Force|pack=Cay Qel-Droma}}</ref>

Cay Qel-Droma is shown to have a green lightsaber on various occasions, as shown in several images in this article. However, in some instances his lightsaber appears to be blue. This is likely just a coloring error, or something done just for simplicity's sake.
